channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Justice Department is expected to slash public corruption unit, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prosecutors in the Justice Department section that handles public corruption cases have been told the unit will be significantly reduced in size, and its cases will be transferred to U.S. attorney's offices around the country, two people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels say 'any Israeli vessel' in nearby Mideast waterways again a target
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels warned shippers early Wednesday that “any Israeli vessel” traveling through nearby Mideast waters is now a target as Israel continues to block aid to the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Missing American college student's hometown sheriff names person of interest five days into search
item.description: The Loudoun County Sheriff’s Office in Virginia named Joshua Riibe, 24, as a "person of interest" in the disappearance of University of Pittsburgh student, Sudiksha Konanki.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump halts doubling of tariffs on Canadian metals after Ontario suspends electricity price hikes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's threat Tuesday to double his planned tariffs on steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% for Canada led the provincial government of Ontario to suspend its planned surcharges on electricity sold to the United States.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: One cat's incredible reunion with its owner after the LA wildfires
item.description: It appears the tall tale that all cats have nine lives may be true for a California Maine coon named Aggie. The beloved feline was feared dead for two months after the Palisades wildfire in Los Angeles left her family's home in ashes. But her owner, 82-year-old Katherine Kiefer, held out some hope.
item.dc:creator: BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Infowars employee killed in Texas, law enforcement confirms
item.description: Jamie White, an Infowars employee, was the victim of a homicide in southeast Austin, Texas, late Sunday night, law enforcement sources confirmed to Nexstar's KXAN.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Hollis, Fox 5

item.title: Garlin Gilchrist announces Democratic bid to become Michigan's first Black governor
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Garlin Gilchrist II, a Democrat from Detroit who is Michigan's first Black lieutenant governor, announced he's jumping into the crowded 2026 race for governor on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police release more details on murder of InfoWars reporter Jamie White as search for suspects continues
item.description: Police in Austin, Texas, believe InfoWars reporter Jamie White was shot and killed when he interrupted suspects possibly burglarizing his car at his apartment complex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: University of Pittsburgh student missing in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: The search involves joint efforts by authorities in the Dominican Republic, the United States and India.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A federal judge has halted Louisiana’s first nitrogen gas execution. The state says it will appeal
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge has halted Louisiana’s first death row execution using nitrogen gas, which was scheduled to take place next week. U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What is known about the deadly January air crash between a passenger jet and US Army helicopter
item.description: Federal aviation officials will continue making sure that planes and helicopters no longer share the same airspace over the nation's capital in the wake of January's midair collision, which killed 67 people and was the deadliest U.S. plane crash in more than two decades.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: White House pressures Columbia University as it seeks to deport pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The White House complained Tuesday that Columbia University is refusing to help federal agents find people being sought as part of the government’s effort to deport participants in pro-Palestinian demonstrations, as the administration continued to punish the school by yanking federal
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Department of Education lays off nearly half of workforce
item.description: The Department of Education announced Tuesday it was firing nearly half of its workforce, the latest Trump administration move to shrink the federal government that could face swift legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 4 charged in death of 5-year-old boy 'incinerated' in hyperbaric chamber explosion
item.description: TROY, Mich. (AP) — Four people have been charged in the death of a 5-year-old boy who was “incinerated” inside a pressurized oxygen chamber that exploded at a suburban Detroit medical facility, Michigan’s attorney general said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about Mahmoud Khalil, the Columbia protester arrested by ICE and facing deportation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Palestinian activist who helped lead student protests at Columbia University faces deportation after being arrested over the weekend by federal immigration officials.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New lawsuit claims Girl Scout Cookies contain 'heavy metals' and pesticides
item.description: A new federal lawsuit claims Girl Scout Cookies contain heavy metals, but the organization assures the cookies are safe.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: House Republicans pass bill to avert government shutdown
item.description: The House on Tuesday passed a funding bill to avert an end-of-the-week government shutdown, teeing up the measure for consideration in the Senate. The chamber cleared the continuing resolution (CR) in a largely party-line 217-213 vote, with just one Democrat — Rep.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KTLA 5

item.title: Medical chopper reported 'flight control problem' before crash that killed 3 on board in Mississippi
item.description: CANTON, Miss. (AP) — A medical helicopter that crashed in a wooded area in Mississippi reported “a flight control problem” shortly before a Monday crash that killed all three people on board, federal authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NTSB urges ban on some helicopter flights at Reagan airport after deadly collision
item.description: Federal investigators looking for the cause of the January collision between a passenger jet and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people recommended a ban on some helicopter flights Tuesday to improve safety.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US bans flights to Haiti's capital until Sept. 8 as UN expert says gang violence is more dire
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Tuesday extended its ban on flights to Haiti’s capital until Sept. 8 because of escalating gang violence, which the U.N.’s human rights expert on the Caribbean nation said is more dire than ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Blizzard Entertainment president says 'there's a game for you' no matter what type of player you are
item.description: IRVINE, Calif. (AP) — California gaming giant Blizzard Entertainment announced Thursday that its popular event BlizzCon is coming back after several years off. The celebration of all things Blizzard, which will be held at the Anaheim Convention Center, is scheduled for September 2026.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 charged in death of 5-year-old boy 'incinerated' in hyperbaric chamber explosion
item.description: “A single spark it appears ignited into a fully involved fire that claimed Thomas’s life within seconds,” Attorney General Dana Nessel said.
item.dc:creator: Daylyn Huff, NewsNation

item.title: Angels, Rangers hats pulled from fan shops over accidental vulgarities
item.description: The MLB regular season will start soon, which means a slew of new merchandise and hats are being released. That includes a line of New Era caps, which, for a couple of teams, have already been removed from their fan shops amid potentially vulgar appearances.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Las Vegas restaurant serving more than just food by helping at-risk young adults
item.description: From the colorful décor to messages of aspirations, Blooming Bistro Co-Owner Samantha Steele wants to serve hope in every bite.
item.dc:creator: Mary Jane Belleza, NewsNation

item.title: An office known for enforcing special education is now focused on Trump's political priorities
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department's Office for Civil Rights is known best for enforcing the right to disability services across America’s schools. But under President Donald Trump, it’s taking a frontline role in his political battles.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former US Rep. Katie Porter steps into crowded California governor's race
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former Democratic U.S. House member Katie Porter announced Tuesday that she is entering the 2026 contest for California governor, joining a crowded field of candidates that could be upended if former Vice President Kamala Harris joins the race.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USDA halts more than $1B in funding for local food banks, schools
item.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) says it has nixed more than $1 billion in funding for local food banks and schools by terminating two programs that aid state, tribal and territorial government food purchases.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Authorities give update on 'wrapped' body found in fishing nets
item.description: No foul play is suspected in the case of the "wrapped" deceased body that was pulled from the ocean by a fishing crew off the coast of Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Thousands at risk due to volcanic eruption in Guatemala
item.description: One of Central America’s most active volcanoes has erupted again, causing evacuations to be ordered near a popular tourist city in Guatemala. The latest eruption of Volcán de Fuego (English: Volcano of Fire) started around 9 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Democrat David Gottfried wins Minnesota House special election, restoring a 67-67 power split
item.description: Voters in a Minnesota House district at the center of a post-election drama have chosen Democrat David Gottfried in a race that leaves control of the House tied.
item.dc:creator: Steve Karnowski, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Southwest Airlines ending free checked luggage policy
item.description: Southwest Airlines will no longer allow bags to fly free. The airline announced Tuesday it will begin charging some customers who check baggage in May.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump considers easing safety screenings for chemicals
item.description: The Trump administration will consider easing regulations under which it considers the safety of existing chemicals, drawing concerns from public health advocates.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KRON4

item.title: Hawks, butterflies and a song for her murdered son help Georgia mom reclaim joy after killings
item.description: ROSWELL, Ga. (AP) — A hawk swooped in and landed on a tree, then watched as Michele and Jeremy Davis mourned their murdered son at a vigil outside his high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
